name: Gravel loop in Saja-Besaya natural park
name_original: Vuelta gravel al parque natural Saja-Besaya
description:
This Route takes you to discover the Saja-Besaya Natural Park, which takes its name from the rivers that delimit it.

The starting and ending point of the route is the town of Ruente, well connected to Cabezón de la Sal, where we can get there by train or bus.

After taking the track that goes up the Bayones river valley, you must turn south and start the first ascent of the day that will take you to the top of the Moral hermitage. As you gain altitude, the landscape unfolds, passing from dense valley bottoms to bare mountains.

After a nice descent towards the Saja river valley, take a stretch of road to Bárcena Mayor. Here begins one of the most spectacular parts of the Route: the climb through the beech forest to the Cruz de Fuentes.

The return, almost all downhill, is going to be done along the beautiful road that winds through the Saja river valley passing through the towns of El Tojo, Renedo and Valle until returning to Ruente.

When you complete the lap, you will have pedaled just over 85 kilometers and added more than 2000 meters of positive elevation gain. It is therefore a route in which good physical preparation is needed to face it safely.

It is an ideal route to do with a gravel bike, as it combines good tracks with sections that run along quiet roads.

I recommend you do the route in autumn, if it is not too cold, to enjoy the beautiful display of colors of the beech forests in their annual leaf shedding.
region_intro: Gravel biking around Tudanca-Cabuerniga offers challenging routes through varied terrain. The region is characterized by its natural parks, including Saja-Besaya and Oyambre, and features coastal sections alongside inland hills. These areas provide a mix of forest paths, open landscapes, and significant elevation changes suitable for experienced gravel riders. The routes often connect natural features with coastal views. Best gravel bike trails around Tudanca-Cabuerniga The most popular gravel bike trail is Gravel loop in Saja-Besaya natural park, a 53.2 miles (85.6 km) trail that takes 8 hours 34 minutes to complete. This difficult route features over 6,700 feet (2,000 meters) of elevation gain through the natural park. Another top favourite among local gravel bikers is San Vicente de la Barquera – Oyambre Natural Park loop from San Vicente de la Barquera, a difficult 32.5 miles (52.3 km) path. This route offers a mix of coastal scenery and natural park landscapes, with over 3,400 feet (1,000 meters) of climbing. Local gravel bikers also love the Buelna Beach – Toró Beach loop from Carranzo, a 52.9 miles (85.2 km) trail leading through coastal areas and inland sections, often completed in about 7 hours 33 minutes. Gravel biking around Tudanca-Cabuerniga is defined by natural park landscapes, challenging climbs, and coastal sections. The network primarily offers difficult routes for experienced riders. The routes in Tudanca-Cabuerniga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 30 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore Tudanca-Cabuerniga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How challenging are the gravel bike trails in Tudanca-Cabuerniga?
A: The gravel bike trails in Tudanca-Cabuerniga are generally challenging, designed for experienced riders. All listed routes are classified as difficult, featuring significant elevation gains and varied terrain through natural parks and coastal sections. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e825025474/gravel-loop-in-saja-besaya-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gravel loop in Saja-Besaya natural park&lt;/a&gt; includes over 6,700 feet (2,000 meters) of climbing.

Q: Are there any family-friendly gravel bike trails in Tudanca-Cabuerniga?
A: Given the challenging nature of the terrain and the significant elevation changes, most gravel bike routes in Tudanca-Cabuerniga are not typically recommended for families with young children or beginners. The routes are primarily suited for experienced gravel riders seeking a demanding adventure.

Q: What are the typical lengths of gravel bike routes in Tudanca-Cabuerniga?
A: The gravel bike routes in Tudanca-Cabuerniga vary in length, generally ranging from around 30 miles (50 km) to over 50 miles (80 km). For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31495203" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Vicente de la Barquera – Oyambre Natural Park loop&lt;/a&gt; is about 32.5 miles (52.3 km), while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40005054" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buelna Beach – Toró Beach loop&lt;/a&gt; covers approximately 52.9 miles (85.2 km).

Q: When is the best time of year to go gravel biking in Tudanca-Cabuerniga?
A: The best time for gravel biking in Tudanca-Cabuerniga is typically during spring, summer, and early autumn. These seasons offer more stable weather conditions, less rain, and pleasant temperatures, making the challenging routes more enjoyable. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: What are the conditions like for gravel biking in Tudanca-Cabuerniga during winter?
A: Winter conditions in Tudanca-Cabuerniga can be challenging for gravel biking. The region, especially in its higher elevations and natural parks, may experience colder temperatures, rain, and potentially snow or ice, making some trails impassable or very difficult. Coastal sections might be more accessible but can still be exposed to strong winds and rain.

Q: Are most gravel bike routes in Tudanca-Cabuerniga circular?
A: Yes, many of the gravel bike routes in Tudanca-Cabuerniga are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e825025474/gravel-loop-in-saja-besaya-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gravel loop in Saja-Besaya natural park&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31495203" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Vicente de la Barquera – Oyambre Natural Park loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What can I see along the gravel bike trails in Tudanca-Cabuerniga?
A: The gravel bike trails in Tudanca-Cabuerniga offer diverse scenery, from the natural parks to coastal views. You might encounter natural landmarks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4243290" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fallen Oak of Brujilices&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4566932" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Moral Plateau&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features charming settlements such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4787483" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bárcena Mayor&lt;/a&gt; and impressive peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839687" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tres Mares Peak&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments along the gravel bike routes?
A: While the routes traverse natural and sometimes remote areas, you will find opportunities for refreshments in the villages and towns along or near the trails. For example, routes passing through or near San Vicente de la Barquera or Carranzo will offer cafes and restaurants. It's always advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer, more remote sections.

Q: Where can I find parking for gravel biking in Tudanca-Cabuerniga?
A: Parking is generally available in the starting points of many routes, often in or near villages. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38151064" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ucieda Parking with Fountain – Collada de Carmona loop&lt;/a&gt; suggests a starting point with parking. Look for designated parking areas in towns like San Vicente de la Barquera, Carranzo, or Ruente, depending on your chosen route.

Q: What do other gravel bikers enjoy the most about gravel biking in Tudanca-Cabuerniga?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the challenging climbs, the stunning mix of natural park landscapes, and the unique coastal sections that define gravel biking in Tudanca-Cabuerniga.

Q: Are there any less-known gravel routes worth exploring?
A: Beyond the most popular options, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38151064" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ucieda Parking with Fountain – Collada de Carmona loop from Ruente&lt;/a&gt; offer a deep dive into the region's interior, featuring significant elevation and immersive natural scenery. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38151017" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de San Antonio – San Ciprián Climb loop from Virgen de la Peña&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a challenging ride through picturesque landscapes.

Q: Can I find wild swimming spots near the gravel bike trails?
A: Yes, the region's natural environment offers opportunities for wild swimming. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4629697" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pool area in the Argonza River&lt;/a&gt; is one such spot where you might cool off after a ride. Always exercise caution and check local regulations before swimming in natural bodies of water.
tips: Beautiful place especially for hot days since you can cool off in one of them. It is reached by a very cool track that runs next to the Argonza River and then continues through the heart of the Saja-Besaya Park.

A delightful section to roll through dense beech forests.
Going through it in autumn is to ensure a festival of colors.
Spectacular and long ascent by rolling track with wonderful views of the Cabuérniga valleys
Nice vantage point right on the road.

waypoints.name: Pool area in the Argonza River
Beautiful section through the beech forest
